age.title: Age
age.description: Patient age at time of sampling.
age.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: age
age.effect_note: In many PDAC risk models, higher age is often associated with higher predicted risk. This app shows a model output, not a diagnosis.

sex.title: Sex
sex.description: Recorded biological sex in the dataset (M/F).
sex.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: sex
sex.effect_note: Categorical input used by the model. Any association depends on the training data distribution.

plasma_CA19_9.title: Plasma CA19-9
plasma_CA19_9.description: Carbohydrate antigen 19-9 (CA19-9) is a serum biomarker commonly measured in pancreatic disease evaluation.
plasma_CA19_9.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: plasma_CA19_9
plasma_CA19_9.effect_note: Higher values can increase predicted risk in many models, but interpretation depends on clinical context and can be elevated for non-cancer reasons.

creatinine.title: Creatinine
creatinine.description: Serum creatinine is a marker related to kidney function and can correlate with patient physiology and lab measurement context.
creatinine.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: creatinine
creatinine.effect_note: Used as an input feature; any relationship to predicted risk is learned from the dataset and is not necessarily causal.

LYVE1.title: LYVE1
LYVE1.description: LYVE1 is a protein biomarker measured in blood in the referenced study.
LYVE1.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: LYVE1
LYVE1.effect_note: The model learns patterns across biomarker levels; direction of effect can vary by model and cohort.

REG1B.title: REG1B
REG1B.description: REG1B is a protein biomarker measured in blood in the referenced study.
REG1B.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: REG1B
REG1B.effect_note: The model uses REG1B jointly with other biomarkers; individual values should not be interpreted in isolation.

TFF1.title: TFF1
TFF1.description: TFF1 is a protein biomarker measured in blood in the referenced study.
TFF1.dataset_source: Debernardi et al. 2020 dataset column: TFF1
TFF1.effect_note: The model uses TFF1 jointly with other biomarkers; changes in predicted risk reflect the learned multivariate pattern.
